titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of TNA DL 36/1/240

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=866 status=live modified=2021-04-07 09:22:01.815219+00 | translation moa=867 status=live modified=2021-04-07 09:22:03.14279+00

p.cl: Vniuersis Christi fidelibus presentes literas visuris uel audituris Humfridus de Boys filius domini Thom’ de Boys salutem in domino

No
ueritis me dedisse concessisse et hac presenti carta mea confirmasse et pro me et heredibus meis imperpetuum quietumclamasse
domino meo domino Roberto de Brus domino uallis Anand’ . Elsystales cum omnibus suis pertinetiis sine ullo retinemento prout di
uiditur a terra mea de Eskeby per fossatum quod dictus dominus meus Ibidem fieri fecit in omnibus locis prout longe lateque uersus meridi
em . austrem . boriam . et orientem se extendit . Tenendum et habendum dicto domino meo et heredibus suis uel suis assignatis libere . quiete . Integre
et pacifice . in boscis . planis . moris . mariscis et locis aliis cum omnibus libertatibus liberis consuetudinibus et asiamentibus ad
Idem locum interius et exterius qualiscumque pertinentibus . Et ego Humfridus et heredes mei predictam terram cum suis pertinenciis ut pre
dictum est predicto domino meo domino Robeto de Brus et heredibus suis contra omnes gentes warantizabimus aquietabimus et inperpetuum
defendemus .
In cuius rei testimonium presentibus sigillum meum apposui .

Testibus . dominis Willelmo de brus . Humfrido de kirkepatric . Da
uid de Torthorald’ . Roberto de heriz . Rogero de kirkepatric . Iohanne de Seton’ . Militibus . Ada de hodolm’ . Hugone Mauleuerer
et aliis .
To all Christ's faithful who shall view or hear the present letters, Humphrey del Bois son of the lord Thomas del Bois sends greeting in the Lord.

Know me to have given, granted, and by this my present charter to have made firm, and for myself and my heirs to have quitclaimed to my lord, the lord Robert de Brus the lord of Annandale, Elshieshiels with all its pertinents, without any withholding, as it is divided by my land of Eskby by the ditch which my said lord had made there, in all places as long and as wide as it extends towards the south, the north, and east. For my said lord and his heirs or assigns to hold and to have freely, quitly, wholly and peaceably, in woods, plains, moors, marshes, and other places, with all liberties, free customs and easements of whatever kind pertaining to the same place within and without. And I Humphrey and my heirs shall warrant, acquit, and protect the aforesaid land for ever, for my aforesaid lord, with its pertinents as said before.
In testimony of this matter I have applied my seal to the presents.
As witnesses, the lords William de Brus, Humphrey of Kirkpatrick, David of Torthorwald, Robert de Heriz, Roger of Kirkpatrick, John of Seton, knights, Adam of Hoddom, Hugh Mauleverer, and others.
